basicDetails.schemeName: State Award To Two Anganwadi Workers Under Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) Schemes

schemeContent.briefDescription: The scheme recognizes Anganwadi Workers under the Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) Schemes for their exemplary performance in improving the quality and coverage of services provided to children and mothers. Each awardee receives a cash award of ₹5,000/- and a citation.
schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: The "State Award To Two Anganwadi Workers Under Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) Schemes" was launched by the Directorate of Women and Child Development (DW&CD), Goa. The scheme aims to recognize the exemplary performance of Anganwadi Workers. The objective is to highlight their success in improving the coverage and quality of services delivered to children and pregnant and lactating mothers under the Integrated Child Development Services Scheme. Furthermore, the scheme recognizes their contribution towards the implementation of various programmes and schemes of the central Government, state Government, or local bodies. The award provides each selected Anganwadi Worker with a cash prize of ₹5,000/- and a citation. The selection process is based on recommendations made by various authorities, which are then forwarded to a Scrutiny Committee.
schemeContent.benefits_md: - Each selected awardee receives a Cash Award of ₹5,000/-. - Each selected awardee receives a citation.

children.text: The "State Award To Two Anganwadi Workers Under Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) Schemes" was launched by the Directorate of Women and Child Development (DW&CD), Goa. The scheme aims to recognize the exemplary performance of Anganwadi Workers. The objective is to highlight their success in improving the coverage and quality of services delivered to children and pregnant and lactating mothers under the Integrated Child Development Services Scheme. Furthermore, the scheme recognizes their contribution towards the implementation of various programmes and schemes of the central Government, state Government, or local bodies. The award provides each selected Anganwadi Worker with a cash prize of ₹5,000/- and a citation. The selection process is based on recommendations made by various authorities, which are then forwarded to a Scrutiny Committee.
Each selected awardee receives a Cash Award of ₹5,000/-.
Each selected awardee receives a citation.
*The process detailed below describes the pathway for nomination and selection, as the Anganwadi Worker does not apply directly for this award.

The recommendation for the award must be initiated by authorized officials or bodies, which include District/Block level Public Representation Institutions (PRIs), Members of Legislative Assembly (MLAs), Members of Parliament (MPs), Women’s Organisations, Child Development Project Officers (CDPO), District Programme Officers of Integrated Child Development Services Scheme, or the District Collector.
The initiated recommendation, detailing the exemplary performance of the Anganwadi Worker, must then be put forward to the designated Scrutiny Committee for further review and selection.

applicationProcess.process_md: ****The process detailed below describes the pathway for nomination and selection, as the Anganwadi Worker does not apply directly for this award.*** **Step 1:** The recommendation for the award must be initiated by authorized officials or bodies, which include District/Block level Public Representation Institutions (PRIs), Members of Legislative Assembly (MLAs), Members of Parliament (MPs), Women’s Organisations, Child Development Project Officers (CDPO), District Programme Officers of Integrated Child Development Services Scheme, or the District Collector. **Step 2:** The initiated recommendation, detailing the exemplary performance of the Anganwadi Worker, must then be put forward to the designated Scrutiny Committee for further review and selection.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: - The applicant must be an Anganwadi Worker under the Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) Schemes. - The applicant must demonstrate exemplary performance in improving the coverage and quality of services provided to children and pregnant and lactating mothers under the Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) Scheme. - The applicant must show contribution towards the implementation of programmes or schemes of the central Government, state Government, or local bodies.

children.text: The applicant must be an Anganwadi Worker under the Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) Schemes.

The applicant must demonstrate exemplary performance in improving the coverage and quality of services provided to children and pregnant and lactating mothers under the Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) Scheme.
The applicant must show contribution towards the implementation of programmes or schemes of the central Government, state Government, or local bodies.
